rationale: Both miss the two concrete reviewer asks (run the diffusion benchmark, fix precommit); X only tangentially touches the parent-order question while Y directly investigates and confirms it via the orchestrator call site, giving Y a modest recall edge. But X's five findings are concretely grounded with file/line evidence and specific proposed fixes (missing outputs[] guard, unused requires_multimodal_data, stale docstring/log text), while Y is mostly a validation narrative with vague or unverifiable claims (asserting CI gates are 'green' while also claiming NPU/AMD CI shows FAILURE, and an unsourced 'author reports' claim) and only two low-actionability non-blocking notes, so X wins on precision and actionability enough to edge out overall.
